source_intake_record.raw_question_text: How many ways are there to choose a white square and a black square, such as those shown, from an $8 \times 8$ chess board so that these squares do not lie in either the same row or the same column? <image1>

image_dependency: Question is self-contained text and does not explicitly refer to a diagram or labeled visual element
An image asset exists in the registry, but the task can be understood from the standard 8x8 chessboard description alone
multi_step: The problem combines multiple constraints: choose one white square and one black square, and exclude pairs in the same row or same column
A correct solution typically requires at least two reasoning steps, such as counting valid opposite-color choices per square or using complementary counting
The 8x8 board structure and color pattern introduce combinatorial reasoning beyond direct lookup
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text is a single explicit value: 768
The counting constraints are precise and rule-based, making the result independently checkable
registry_passed=true, with question and answer both present in the manifests
